projects.title: Hát Bội Saigon Art Cruise
projects.summary: A floating cultural programme that introduces Southern Vietnamese Hát Bội through research, dramaturgy and an audience friendly live narrative.
projects.outcome: A layered public experience that connects Hát Bội research, live performance and accessible interpretation.
projects.article:
Developed for Saigon Art Cruise, the programme brings classical theatre into a contemporary river journey. I shaped the curatorial framing, script and communication materials so that visual codes, character types and performance conventions could be followed by new audiences.

The collection includes visual direction, scripts, performance information, social content, video and event records.
projects.imageAlt: Hát Bội Saigon Art Cruise project collection

media.title: Kich Ban - Tran Hung Dao Bach Dang Giang.docx

projects.title: Khơi Gợi Mỹ Cảm
projects.summary: To understand is the beginning of love. Khơi Gợi Mỹ Cảm is a thematic series exploring the aesthetics of Vietnamese art, from the period before the École des Beaux-Arts de l’Indochine to the contemporary era.
projects.outcome: A research led public programme that turns art history into an accessible, contemporary encounter for younger audiences.
projects.article:
Created for younger audiences, the series builds an approachable path into Vietnamese art history and encourages a more personal sensitivity to the beauty of the country’s visual culture.

Researcher Lý Đợi guides the programme, connecting historical context, close looking and conversation across different periods of Vietnamese art.
projects.imageAlt: Khơi Gợi Mỹ Cảm project collection

projects.title: Đờn Ca Tài Tử Culture Class
projects.summary: A participatory cultural class introducing Đờn Ca Tài Tử through vọng cổ and the development of Southern Vietnamese cải lương.
projects.outcome: A complete class record connecting musical forms, historical context and an audience centred learning experience.
projects.article:
The class combines listening, musical context and guided discussion to make the relationship between Đờn Ca Tài Tử, vọng cổ and Southern cải lương easier to recognise.

Curriculum, agenda, participant material and event photography preserve both the learning structure and the atmosphere of the programme.
projects.imageAlt: Đờn Ca Tài Tử Culture Class project collection

media.title: NX ASIA Giáo án Đờn ca tài tử.pdf
KÍNH CHÚC. Tam pháp nhập môn.docx

projects.title: Tranh Gói Vải Culture Class
projects.summary: A hands on culture class exploring the material language and traditional practice of fabric wrapped pictures.
projects.outcome: A compact learning format that translates a material tradition into an approachable making experience.
projects.article:
The programme invites participants to learn through making, bringing close attention to fabric, folding, composition and the cultural context of the form.

The collection preserves the project’s core visual communication and public facing framing.
projects.imageAlt: Tranh Gói Vải Culture Class project collection

projects.title: Improv Kịch Ứng Tác
projects.summary: An audience facing improvised theatre programme built around spontaneity, participation and the shared energy of live performance.
projects.outcome: Editorial framing and live documentation that present improvisation as an immediate, participatory cultural experience.
projects.article:
Landing page copy and programme framing communicate the open, responsive nature of improvisation without over explaining the experience.

Event photography records the performers, audience interaction and changing rhythm of the live programme.
projects.imageAlt: Improv Kịch Ứng Tác project collection

projects.title: Lọng Bướm
projects.summary: A project retracing Lọng Bướm, a distinctive decorative parasol once used by the An Nam people, through an interpretive reconstruction based on derivative sources rather than an original artefact or primary record.
projects.outcome: An interpretive reconstruction that makes a little known decorative object legible while remaining transparent about the limits of the surviving evidence.
projects.article:
The project carefully distinguishes phỏng dựng from phục dựng: it proposes a new physical interpretation from secondary and derivative material, rather than claiming to restore an original object.

Researchers and artists Khoa Phùng, Nghiêu Thiên and Lâm Ơi guided the process. Interviews, exhibition writing, press material, video and installation records document both the research and the public presentation.
projects.imageAlt: Lọng Bướm project collection

media.title: 19 câu hỏi phỏng vấn nghệ sĩ làm lọng_1.docx

projects.title: Người Đơm Hoa Giấy
projects.summary: A cultural exhibition dedicated to preserving and continuing the paper flower craft of Thanh Tiên village in Huế.
projects.outcome: An exhibition record that supports the continuity of a Huế craft village by centring its makers, techniques and cultural memory.
projects.article:
The project follows the makers, materials and inherited knowledge behind Thanh Tiên paper flowers, treating the craft as a living practice rather than a nostalgic object.

Landing page material and a full photographic record document the project from narrative development to exhibition and public encounter.
projects.imageAlt: Người Đơm Hoa Giấy project collection

projects.title: Vàng Sơn Một Thuở
projects.summary: Một Thuở Vàng Son uses the language of sculpture to tell stories from tuồng, also known as Hát Bội.
projects.outcome: A sculptural interpretation that carries the narrative world of tuồng into a contemporary exhibition setting.
projects.article:
The exhibition translates the figures, gestures and dramatic memory of tuồng into sculptural form. Editorial framing, artist context and spatial planning help audiences move between traditional theatre and contemporary exhibition language.

Exhibition visuals, press material, artist documentation and display proposals are preserved together in this collection.
projects.imageAlt: Vàng Sơn Một Thuở project collection

projects.title: Hát Bội Cultural Programme
projects.summary: A public programme introducing Hát Bội in relation to the performative and ceremonial world of Huế court music, Nhã nhạc cung đình Huế.
projects.outcome: A substantial visual record of traditional performance presented as a living, audience facing cultural practice.
projects.article:
The programme creates an accessible point of entry into classical Vietnamese performance through costume, gesture, music and live encounters.

More than thirty selected photographs document performers, staging, ceremonial detail and audience experience across multiple sessions.
projects.imageAlt: Hát Bội Cultural Programme project collection

projects.title: Toong Brand Copywriting System
projects.summary: Editorial and campaign copy across social media, internal communication, email marketing, factsheets, brochures and video.
projects.outcome: A cross channel brand system spanning campaign scripts, sales materials and customer communication.
projects.article:
At Toong, copywriting extended beyond campaign headlines. I worked across social media, internal communication, email marketing, landing pages, factsheets, brochures and launch materials.

The central task was consistency: each format needed its own pace and level of detail while still sounding like the same brand.
projects.imageAlt: Toong Brand Copywriting System project collection

projects.title: Cuộc Đua Đầu Thai
projects.summary: A folklore inspired board game concept built around a race board, eight supernatural characters and a playful narrative system.
projects.outcome: Race board, character set and rule document form a complete playable concept archive.
projects.article:
This personal design exercise explores Vietnamese supernatural folklore through a compact race game.

The project combines character concepts, a shared race board and a playful reincarnation narrative.
